commentary: Wrapped my third year in the Wake Tech carpentry apprenticeship. One more to go before I sit for journeyman. Grateful to the crew at Ridgeline for the reps.
Another Wake Forest kitchen reno handed off this month. Custom cabinetry and full trim package. Proud of the crew.
Applications are open for the fall carpentry apprenticeship cohort. Earn while you learn a skilled trade. Details on our site.
First solo trim job start to finish this week. Doors, casing, base, the works. Came out clean.
Proud of this year's apprentices. Watching them go from framing squares to finished millwork never gets old.
Now booking spring renovation projects across Wake County. Kitchens, baths, and additions. Reach out for an estimate.
The No. 7 jointer plane is back in stock. A honed sole and a sharp iron will get you a joint that closes with no glue line.
Fresh load of 4/4 white oak and walnut just hit the racks. Come pick through it before the cabinet shops clean us out.
Reminder to my apprentices: measure the opening three times before you cut the casing. Trim never lies about a sloppy layout.
Dust collection is not optional. Cleaner air on the jobsite and a cleaner finish on the work. Pair the sander with the extractor.
Deck season is coming. If you're planning an outdoor build this year, now is the time to get on the schedule.
Congratulations to our carpentry apprentices completing their coursework this term. The trades need you.
Finished a walnut built-in this week. Hand-cut dovetails on the drawers because the client noticed the little things. Worth it.
New router table fence system in stock. Micro-adjust and dust collection built in. A shop upgrade worth making.
The skilled trades gap is real. Supporting apprenticeships is how we build the next generation of builders in North Carolina.
Timber Creek back in the taproom rotation this spring. Come out, grab a beer, hear some songs. Dates going up soon.
Cold weather is hard on batteries. Keep your packs off the concrete and out of the truck bed overnight this winter.
Kiln-dried cherry, maple, and ash in stock in Gibsonville. Great time to stock up before your winter shop projects.
If you're a carpenter looking for steady work around the Triangle, there are more openings than guys right now. Reach out.
Sharpening class this month at the Raleigh store. Bring your dull chisels and plane irons and leave with a shaving-sharp edge.
